steps_raw.instruction: Place 50-100 mg of fecal sample into tube.
Add 1 mL of PBS per 100 mg of feces.
Mix thoroughly by vortexing.
Allow the sample to stand for 00:02:00 to sediment large debris.
Transfer 300 µL of the suspension to 1.5 mL tube.
Centrifuge at 10000 x g, °C, 3 minutes .
Discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet (~ 30 mg of feces) in 300 µL of PBS.
Incubate at 70 °C for 00:10:00 on the block heater.

Transfer 300 µL of the suspension to EZ-beads tube. The EZ-Beads tube contains zirconium oxide beads of two different sizes (0.2 mm spheres and a large 5 mm bead) that can facilitate efficient cell lysis by bead beating.
Lyse cells either by using disruption device (12.1) or vortex mixer (12.2).

Place the EZ-beads tube in Micro Smash instrument and disrupt cells by shaking at 2500 rpm, °C, 2 minutes . Micro Smash Beads Cell Disrupter (TOMY Digital Biology, MS-100) Caution: Avoid using a disruption device with a high-speed linear reciprocating motion, as this may potentially result in breakage of the EZ-Beads tubes.

Place the EZ-beads tube on MN Bead Tube Holder attached to Vortex-Genie mixer and vortex for 00:05:00 at maximum speed. MN Bead Tube Holder (Rubber-foam adapter for processing bead tubes with Vortex-Genie instrument, MACHEREY-NAGEL, 740469)
Briefly spin the tube to collect contents.
Add 300 µL of Lysis Buffer and 30 µL of Proteinase K Solution to the sample in EZ-beads tube.
Mix by inverting the tube.
Incubate at 56 °C for 00:20:00 on the block heater.
Briefly spin the tube.
Transfer the supernatant (~ 500 µL ) to 1.5 mL tube.
Centrifuge at 18000 x g, °C, 3 minutes .
Transfer the cleared lysate to Maxwell RSC Cartridge.
Add 50 µL of Elution Buffer to elution tube.
Start the extraction run following the manufacturer's instructions. Maxwell RSC instrument (Automated nucleic acid purification platform, Promega, AS4500)
Cool to Room temperature .
Briefly spin the tube.
